acceptance_criteria: CodeClash is checked out at commit 381cdfa05a35e8acd35853b9fc7e13005121b127.
The Telos BattleSnake deterministic edit config runs without provider credentials.
Metadata contains parseable BattleSnake round statistics.
p1 has a Mini-SWE-Agent trajectory artifact.
p1 agent_stats record zero provider cost and at least two deterministic model invocations.
p1 diff-scope evidence is non-empty and names telos_marker.py.
No model capability, leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain result is claimed.

evidence.notes: Raw GitHub Actions artifact bundle is committed, including run log, metadata, p1 trajectory, player change records, logs, and round archives.
The GitHub workflow completed successfully and the summarizer recorded zero provider cost with two deterministic model invocations.
p1 change record contains a non-empty diff creating telos_marker.py; p2 remains empty as the dummy control.
Review limits the claim to deterministic non-empty diff evidence capture and rejects provider-model, leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, and live-domain claims.
No production or live-domain behavior changed.

falsifiers: Receipt fails if the workflow did not complete successfully.
Receipt fails if CodeClash was not checked out at the frozen commit.
Receipt fails if metadata lacks parseable BattleSnake round statistics.
Receipt fails if p1 trajectory or agent_stats are missing.
Receipt fails if deterministic model invocations are fewer than two.
Receipt fails if provider cost is nonzero.
Receipt fails if p1 diff-scope evidence is absent, empty, or does not name telos_marker.py.
Receipt fails if the result claims model capability, leaderboard performance, SWE-bench performance, or production/live-domain verification.
stated_goal: Run the selected deterministic Mini-SWE-Agent BattleSnake edit smoke and verify that Telos can capture a real non-empty p1 code diff, trajectory, agent_stats, artifact, and review evidence without provider model spend.
